blockingIssues.description: PendingApprovalsPanel SIGN preview extraction reads only context.message_hex_32/message_hex, but real runtime pending SIGN context is nested SignSession data, so contract-required message previews are omitted for real pending operations.
PolicyPromptModal remainingMs state is not reset per queued prompt, so FIFO queue advancement can inherit stale/zero TTL and auto-dismiss subsequent prompts incorrectly.
Multi-tab sync broadcasts only prompt dismissal by id; policy decisions are not propagated to other tabs, so cross-tab policy state does not converge as expected.
"Always allow" overrides are applied only to in-memory runtime state and are lost across lock/unlock because updated overrides are not persisted before runtime teardown.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align ui-screen-worker procedure with mission AGENTS policy for agent-browser usage on UI changes, and require explicit deviations when browser verification is skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m2 review reports observed AGENTS mandates agent-browser for UI validation while ui-screen-worker allows skipping for data-wiring cases, resulting in inconsistent followedProcedure reporting.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add shared factual documentation for runtime pending_operations context shapes (especially SIGN SignSession payload paths) so UI parsing/tests align with real runtime data.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m2-pending-approvals-panel review found implementation/tests assumed top-level message_hex fields, but captured runtime evidence used nested SignSession context.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the allow-once rollback note to match implementation semantics (unset rollback) or update implementation to match documented deny rollback; keep doc and behavior consistent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m2-reactive-policy-prompt-modal review found docs claim deny rollback while AppStateProvider currently rolls back to unset on lock.
